name: GLiNER (Generalist NER)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); multiple pretrained models on HuggingFace Hub

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub token for model download

performance.notes: Bi-encoder architecture; fast inference; ONNX export available

agent_readiness.notes: Zero-shot Named Entity Recognition model. Unlike spaCy/NLTK which require training for new entity types, GLiNER recognizes ANY entity type by passing labels at inference time. BERT-based bi-encoder architecture. Models available on HuggingFace Hub (multilingual, domain-specific). Replaces expensive LLM calls for structured information extraction. Simple API: model.predict_entities(text, labels=['person', 'organization', 'custom_type']). Excellent for document parsing in agent pipelines.

gotchas: Quality varies by entity type — common entities (person, org) better than niche types
Bi-encoder architecture less accurate than generative LLM extraction for complex cases
Max token length limited (512 tokens typical — chunk long documents)
best_when: Zero-shot NER for custom entity types without training data
High-throughput structured information extraction from documents
Replacing LLM-based extraction with a faster, cheaper model
Agent pipelines parsing text for structured entities
avoid_when: When highest accuracy requires LLM extraction (use GPT-4 with structured outputs)
summary: GLiNER is a zero-shot NER model that recognizes any entity type by passing labels at inference time — no training required for new entity types. BERT-based bi-encoder. Simple API: predict_entities(text, labels=['person', 'product', 'custom']). Excellent for agent pipelines needing fast, cheap structured information extraction from text without LLM costs.
